mechanism: Additive anticoagulation and antiplatelet effects increase GI and intracranial bleeding risk
clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination unless essential; monitor INR and bleeding signs closely

mechanism: NSAID-related platelet dysfunction and GI mucosal injury with possible CYP2C9 interaction increase bleeding
clinical_recommendation: Prefer safer analgesic; if unavoidable, increase INR and bleeding monitoring

mechanism: Renal function decline after iodinated contrast can precipitate metformin-associated lactic acidosis
clinical_recommendation: Hold metformin around contrast exposure and reassess renal function before restart

mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition elevates simvastatin concentration, increasing myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk
clinical_recommendation: Use lower statin dose or alternative statin and monitor CK/muscle symptoms

mechanism: Additive RAAS blockade and potassium retention can cause life-threatening hyperkalemia
clinical_recommendation: Monitor potassium and renal function frequently; avoid in high-risk patients

mechanism: P-glycoprotein inhibition increases digoxin exposure and toxicity risk
clinical_recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose and monitor levels/ECG

mechanism: CYP2C19 inhibition reduces clopidogrel activation and antiplatelet efficacy
clinical_recommendation: Use pantoprazole or separate gastroprotection strategy

mechanism: Excess serotonergic activity increases serotonin syndrome risk
clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination or monitor for agitation, hyperreflexia, and autonomic instability

mechanism: CYP1A2 inhibition raises theophylline levels and toxicity risk
clinical_recommendation: Monitor serum levels and reduce theophylline dose

mechanism: Reduced renal clearance increases methotrexate exposure and hematologic toxicity
clinical_recommendation: Avoid chronic co-administration; monitor CBC and renal function

mechanism: Reduced renal lithium clearance increases neurotoxicity risk
clinical_recommendation: Avoid NSAID or monitor lithium level closely after initiation

mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition increases phenytoin concentration and toxicity
clinical_recommendation: Check phenytoin levels and adjust dose

mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces contraceptive hormone exposure and efficacy
clinical_recommendation: Use additional non-hormonal contraception during and after therapy

mechanism: Additive QT interval prolongation increases torsades de pointes risk
clinical_recommendation: Avoid if possible and monitor ECG/electrolytes

mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition increases carbamazepine levels and CNS toxicity
clinical_recommendation: Use alternative macrolide or monitor levels closely

mechanism: Additive AV nodal suppression can cause bradycardia and heart block
clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination unless specialist-supervised with ECG monitoring

mechanism: Excess cGMP-mediated vasodilation can trigger profound hypotension
clinical_recommendation: Contraindicated: do not co-administer

mechanism: Additive CNS and respiratory depression
clinical_recommendation: Avoid or use minimum doses with close respiratory monitoring

mechanism: Additive ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity potential
clinical_recommendation: Avoid where possible and monitor renal function/hearing

mechanism: Chelation reduces doxycycline gastrointestinal absorption
clinical_recommendation: Separate dosing by at least 2 to 4 hours

mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition increases warfarin effect and INR
clinical_recommendation: Pre-emptive warfarin dose reduction with frequent INR checks

mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition markedly increases tacrolimus exposure
clinical_recommendation: Major tacrolimus dose reduction and therapeutic drug monitoring required

mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition elevates simvastatin concentrations and rhabdomyolysis risk
clinical_recommendation: Temporarily hold simvastatin or switch statin; monitor muscle toxicity

mechanism: Additive potassium retention can precipitate severe hyperkalemia
clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination where possible; monitor potassium and renal function

mechanism: MAO inhibition with serotonergic antidepressant raises serotonin syndrome risk
clinical_recommendation: Avoid concomitant use or provide specialist-monitored washout strategy
